evals.name: bootstrap-from-scratch
evals.prompt: Ya aprobé el requirements.md y el design.md del spec que está en <SANDBOX>/2026-07-02-register-expenses/. Todavía no existe tasks.md. Necesito el plan de tareas para implementarlo: créalo e itera cada tarea hasta que el plan completo esté validado contra el spec y el estado real del código de este repo.
evals.expected_output: A tasks.md created in the sandbox spec folder following the project template (Task overview, Requirements coverage, detailed tasks), where a bootstrap planner ran first and then every task was individually iterated to CRITERIA MET; a final report stating 100% of tasks converged and leaving approval to the user.
files: sandbox copy of docs/specs/2026-07-02-register-expenses without tasks.md
assertions: tasks.md was created in the spec folder and follows the project template: it has a Task overview section, a Requirements coverage table, and one detailed entry per task
The process started with exactly ONE bootstrap planner invocation, followed by per-task iterations (report shows bootstrap first, then one invocation per task, sequentially)
Every task in the final tasks.md was individually iterated to an explicit CRITERIA MET verdict (or an explicit escalation with a reason) — no task was skipped
The Requirements coverage table maps every numbered acceptance criterion present in requirements.md to at least one task
Decision log and Outcome fields of every task are left empty — the run planned only and did not implement or pretend to execute anything
Task overview, detailed task entries, and Requirements coverage table reference a consistent set of task IDs (no orphan or missing IDs)

evals.name: iterate-existing-tasks
evals.prompt: El spec en <SANDBOX>/2026-07-02-register-expenses/ ya tiene un tasks.md en draft con 9 tareas (T1–T9). Audita e itera cada una de las tareas hasta que todas cumplan los criterios de planeación, teniendo en cuenta el estado actual del código del repo.
evals.expected_output: Every task T1–T9 (plus any splits) individually evaluated by a planner subagent, edits applied to tasks.md keeping overview/coverage in sync, and a final report with per-task verdicts showing 100% CRITERIA MET or explicit escalations.
files: sandbox copy of docs/specs/2026-07-02-register-expenses with current draft tasks.md
assertions: The run went straight to per-task iteration (no bootstrap): the report shows one planner invocation per existing task, covering ALL of T1–T9 plus any tasks created by splits
Every task received an explicit verdict, and 100% ended in CRITERIA MET or an explicit escalation with a stated reason (e.g. hit the iteration cap or needs a user decision)
Invocations were sequential and scoped to one task each (no batch like 'evaluate T4-T9 together')
After the edits, the Task overview, the detailed task entries, and the Requirements coverage table are still in sync (same set of task IDs everywhere)
The Requirements coverage table still maps every numbered acceptance criterion from requirements.md to at least one task
No implementation happened: no source files were created or modified in the repo; only tasks.md in the sandbox spec folder changed
